summary: Keyword co-occurrence in bundled CLI without traced installer-harm
details: Pattern rules matched on the bundled CLI files (dist/cli.cjs and dist/cli.js) for co-occurrence of the strings 'ping' and 'POST' within the same minified bundle. These tokens are common in CLI tooling (e.g., HTTP health checks, telemetry, command names) and the matches alone do not establish exfiltration. No hardcoded attacker-controlled C2 endpoint, credential-path read, env-var scrape, lifecycle script (preinstall/install/postinstall), or import-time side effect has been identified. Without a concrete attacker-benefit mechanism in the traced code, the signal is below the block threshold; routing to human review for semantic verification of what the bundled CLI actually does at the flagged offsets.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
